mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market, which underpins strong consumer confidence and spending, ultimately supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where UNRATE is below its 10-year average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate indicates an aggressive monetary easing stance by the central bank. This action typically lowers borrowing costs, stimulates economic activity, and boosts equity valuations by reducing discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances following a triggered FEDFUNDS drop.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in both retail sales and housing starts signifies broad-based strength in consumer demand and investment. This dual strength indicates a healthy economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 2%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing and overall supply-side activity. This deceleration indicates reduced corporate output and potential for lower earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns, especially when production growth is already low.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO growth falls below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects an expanding economy with increasing aggregate demand. This environment generally fosters higher corporate revenues and profits, providing a supportive backdrop for positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where GDPC1 shows positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate indicates a deteriorating labor market, signaling job losses and reduced consumer purchasing power. This leads to lower corporate revenues and profits, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following such an increase in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle aimed at cooling an overheating economy. This typically leads to higher discount rates, increased cost of capital, and reduced corporate profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: In a strong labor market, accelerating payroll employment indicates robust economic momentum and consumer confidence. This translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns, with the effect being particularly pronounced when unemployment is already low.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE is below 4%.
